entry.title: Re: Transport Request Error Object TVDIR,TDDAT cannot be locked
entry.content:
It's not very clear what means "While locking object in transport request". What did you do exactly? SE10 > Menu Transport Request > Object List > Lock? On the task or on the transport request?

I guess it's the message TK022. It's sent by the function module TRINT_LOCK_OBJECT.

NB: concerned messages in plain text for easier Web text indexing:

  • Object R3TR TABU TDDAT cannot be locked
  • Object R3TR TABU TVDIR cannot be locked

TDDAT and TVDIR concern the Table Maintenance Dialogs (generated by TMG).

entry.title: Workflow for Sales quotations and Sales Orders triggered when the end user changes the Item Price
entry.content:
This requirement is to be implemented in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

To trigger a workflow in the Sales Quotation and Sales Order applications, a condition must be checked for the item price. The workflow should be triggered only if the item price has been changed by the end user. To achieve this, a sales quotation or sales order simulation needs to be executed to determine the system-calculated item price. This value must then be compared with the price entered or modified by the user. If a difference is identified, the workflow should be triggered accordingly.

The main challenge is retrieving the item price within the custom logic. However, this can be addressed by calling the Sales Order Simulation API, which provides the calculated item price for comparison.

Pre-requisties : 

  • Required Business Roles must be assigned to the user. 
  • Workflows for Sales Orders must be configured and activated in the system. Refer SAP Help on flexible workflow setup.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/5a5f3f686c06490893a588c99b36adca.html

Solution: 

When sales quotation and sales order created/changed in S/4 system then the Business Add-In (BAdI) SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON is provided to specify whether a sales document is relevant for approvals or not. Refer below documentation for this BADI.  https://api.sap.com/badi/SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON

In this Blog, Sales order is used for reference to explain the use case. 

Communication Setup:

  1. In order to execute API for simulation of the creation of sales order inside Cloud Badi implementation (SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON),  required communcation arrangement to be setup for the Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0109 (Sales Order Integration) to technically enable and activate the Sales Order simulation API - 'API_SALESORDERSIMULATION'.  Refer API Hub for detail :  https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_SALESORDERSIMULATION_0001-v1/overview
  2. Refer SAP Help for guidance on setting up the communication arrangement, system and communication user.   https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/2e84a10c430645a88bdbfaaa23ac9ff7.html
  3. After setup of communication arrangement, we will have the Sales Order Simulation API endpoint to be used inside Custom BADI.
  4.  Since, the Sales order simulation API to be called within the S/4 system itself, so custom communcation scenario to be setup to enable the call of inbound APIs inside Cloud Badi.  In custom communcation scenario, generic outbound service for REST type to be added. Communication arrangement to be created for this custom communication scenario to activate HTTP service with same outbound user as was used in step 1(setup of standard communcation scenrio).  Refer SAP Help document for additional details.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/8d5d71252edd449982083cb5a3b8c8c4-182.html
  5. After completing the above steps, custom communication scenario, outbound service and Sales order simulation API is ready to be used inside implementaton of cloud BADI SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON."

Cloud BADI implementation

  1. Create Implementation of Cloud BADI 'SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON' via Custom Logic app (Key User Extensibility).
  2. Add required checks based on importing parameters - SALESDOCUMENT(Sales Document for Approvals)  and  SALESDOCUMENTITEM (Sales Document Item Table for Approvals). For instance : type of order, sales origanzation, sales document categoty.
  3. Configuration check of communication arrangment, service activation and API Call request-response. 
  • Check if outbound service exists in the communication scenario.

                      CHECK cl_ble_http_client=>is_service_available(
                             communication_scenario = 'YY1_COMM_SCENARIO'
                              outbound_service = 'YY1_COMM_OUTBOUND_REST'
                                    ) = abap_true.

  •         Creation of the HTTP client to access the outbound service
               DATA(lo_client) = cl_ble_http_client=>create(
               communication_scenario = 'YY1_COMM_SCENARIO'
              outbound_service = 'YY1_COMM_OUTBOUND_REST'
                 ).
  •          Creation of service request and set properties  
             *method used for service call
       lo_request = cl_ble_http_request=>create( ).                                                                            
       lo_request->set_header_parameter( EXPORTING name = 'x-csrf-token' value = lv_csrf ).
       lo_request->set_content_type( 'application/json' ).

             * Post method to execute Sales Order Simulation API. URL here is endpoint ('/api_salesordersimulation/srvd_a2x/sap/salesordersimulation/0001/SalesOrder' )
           lo_request->set_method( 'POST' )->set_resource_extension( lv_url ).

* Payload in JSON to be created for passing Sales order header data from SALESDOCUMENT and item data from SALESDOCUMENTITEM.

           Sample Payload :                     

{
  "SalesOrderType": "TA",
  "SalesOrganization": "XXXX",
  "DistributionChannel": "10",
  "OrganizationDivision": "10",
  "SoldToParty": "XXXXXXXXX",
  "_Item": [
    {
       "Product": "XXXX",
      "RequestedQuantity": 1,
      "RequestedQuantityISOUnit" : "PCE"
    },
        {    
      "Product": "XXXX",
      "RequestedQuantity": 2,
      "RequestedQuantityISOUnit" : "PCE"}
  ]  }
  • Call the request by passing Payload created and get the response. 
        lo_request->set_body( lv_payload ).
    * ***Send API request
    lo_response = lo_client->send( lo_request ).
    ***Getting response body from response received
    DATA(lv_body) = lo_response->get_body( ).                                                                                                  
  • From the API response, the value of 'TotalNetAmount' needs to be retrieved.
    The standard class method /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize should be used by passing the response body as input and extracting the value of 'TotalNetAmount' from the resulting structure.

Note: The custom wrapper class is created in ADT(Developer extensbility) to be called inside Cloud logic. This customer class used standard method /ui2/cl_json->deserialize_json to extract the attribute value of  'TotalNetAmount'.

/ui2/cl_json->deserialize_json(EXPORTING
im_payload = lv_body
IMPORTING
ex_total_val = DATA(lv_field_amt) ).

  • Compare the TotalNetAmount entered by the user during Sales Order creation (importing parameter: salesdocument-totalnetamount) with the Total Net Amount retrieved from the API response.  If there is a difference, set the changing parameter SALESDOCAPPROVALREASON  accordingly.

Conclusion : Additional specific complex conditions can be determined inside custom logic to check if Sales order workflow to be triggered or not. For instance, POST operation(simlulation mode) for released API's  can be called to validate conditions inside Custom Logic(Key user extensibility) in S/4 HANA Public Cloud System.

entry.title: Re: Source of Information for Field in structures FPAYH-FPAYHX-FPAYP
entry.content: REF++ fields (FPAYHX-REF01-15 and FPAYP-REF01-05) are filled in Event 05 of Payment Media Workbench (transaction OBPM1 -> Event Modules). These fields are format-specific. Similarly ZREF++ fields are filled in Event 06 and are available for custom enhancements of standard formats (transaction OBPM3 -> Event Modules).

entry.title: Re: Standard report or tile for invoicing plan
entry.content: No standard report exists that directly shows supplier invoicing plans with dates/status from FPLT/FPLA. The recommended approach is to use Custom Analytical Queries or Custom CDS views to expose the invoicing plan data, and optionally integrate with SAC for advanced reporting.

entry.title: Avoiding HANA Connection Exhaustion: A Practical Guide to Hikari & Multi-Tenant Scaling
entry.content:
Introduction

Modern SAP applications running on SAP BTP with CAP Java and SAP HANA rely heavily on efficient database connection management.

Opening a new database connection for every request is expensive:

  • It increases latency
  • It consumes database resources
  • It reduces scalability

To solve this, we use connection pools.

In this blog, we’ll walk through how connection pooling works in CAP Java, what can go wrong in multi-tenant systems, and how to configure it correctly to avoid production outages.

What is a Connection Pool?

A connection pool is a cache of reusable database connections maintained by your application.

open connection → execute query → close connection

becomes:

borrow connection → execute query → return to pool

Benefits

  • Faster response times
  • Reduced database load
  • Reuse of expensive DB sessions
  • Better scalability

How CAP Java Uses Connection Pools

In CAP Java:

  • Database access uses JDBC connection pooling via Spring Boot
  • The default pool implementation is HikariCP
  • Each application instance maintains its own connection pool

This means that if your application is scaled horizontally, the number of connections increases with the number of instances.

Why This Becomes Critical in Production

In the basic mode, the HANA database is provisioned with 2 vCPUs, which allows roughly 1000 concurrent connections.

Now consider a typical CAP multi-tenant setup:

  • Multiple services
  • Multiple tenants
  • Multiple app instances (autoscaling)
  • Each instance maintaining its own pool

The total DB connections can grow very quickly if not controlled.

Multi-Tenant Scaling Problem

Without optimization, the connection count grows like this:

connections = instances × tenants × pool size

Example:

  • 4 tenants
  • 3 application instances
  • default pool size = 10
4 × 3 × 10 = 120 connections

Now multiply this across multiple services and sidecars, you can quickly reach the HANA limit.

The Solution: Shared Pools with combinePools

CAP Java provides a very important optimization:

cds:
  multi-tenancy:
    datasource:
      combinePools:
        enabled: true

This ensures that instead of creating pools per tenant, the application uses:

One shared connection pool per HANA database per application instance

Now the formula becomes:

connections = instances × pool size

This makes connection usage predictable and scalable.

HikariCP Configuration (Production)

A production-ready CAP Java configuration looks like this:

cds:
  multi-tenancy:
    datasource:
      combinePools:
        enabled: true
      hikari:
        maximum-pool-size: 40
        minimum-idle: 10
        idle-timeout: 600000
        max-lifetime: 3300000

Parameter Description

maximumPoolSizeMaximum connections per instance
minimumIdleWarm connections kept ready
idleTimeoutIdle connection eviction time
maxLifetimeMaximum lifetime before connection refresh

How to Size Your Pool Correctly

Use this simple guideline:

maximumPoolSize ≈ concurrent DB requests per instance

And always validate:

instances × maximumPoolSize < HANA DB connection limit

For example:

  • 3 instances
  • pool size 40
3 × 40 = 120 connections

Well within a 1000 connection limit.

Monitoring & Observability

You should continuously monitor:

  • Active DB connections
  • Idle connections
  • Pool utilization
  • Total HANA connection usage

Set alerts at 70–80% of DB capacity.

Common Pitfalls

  • Not enabling combinePools in multi-tenant apps
  • Oversizing connection pools
  • Multiple services sharing same HANA DB without coordination
  • No monitoring of DB connection usage
  • Assuming default values are safe for production

Summary

Area Recommendation

Connection PoolUse HikariCP
Multi-TenancyEnable combinePools
Pool SizingBased on concurrency
MonitoringMandatory
ScalingValidate against DB limits

Final Thoughts

Connection pooling is often invisible, until it fails in production.

With the right configuration in CAP Java and SAP HANA, you can:

  • avoid outages
  • improve performance
  • scale safely in multi-tenant environments

If you are running CAP Java in production, take a few minutes to review your pool configuration, it can prevent your next incident.

References

  • HikariCP
  • DB Connection Pooling
  • CAP Datasource Configuration

entry.title: Re: Change History Log of Debug Mode SE16H for Audit
entry.content:
SE16H which is a new HANA-optimized data browser is primarily designed for read-only analytical queries with advanced filtering and joins. It does not provide the same update/debug functionality as SE16N. Even if debug mode is technically invoked, there is no standard logging mechanism like SE16N_CD_KEY for SE16H. In practice, SE16H is not intended for direct data manipulation, so SAP has not delivered a change log table for it.

As a part of Governance Control, restrict debug mode usage in production systems. Limit SE16N/SE16H authorizations to read-only for auditors and key-users. For sensitive tables, enforce changes only via proper transactions or change documents.

entry.title: Re: Receiving quantities with the TEST RF ENVIRONMENT app...
entry.content:
Good day @Beatriz96 

Thank you for your question.

Receive Products and Test RF Environment are based on different frameworks. The GTIN Scan Incr Count checkbox is a feature of the Receive Product app. In EWM RF, barcode scanning is supported mainly for identification and verification. Fields like CHQTY are designed for numeric quantity entry, not for GTIN entry. See Radio Frequency Framework for more details.

In EWM, GTIN scanning works only if the RF step provides a product identification field and the GTIN is maintained as a valid product identifier.

Question: My question is, what is the configuration in CBC or what check mark do I need to enable so that the "TEST RF ENVIRONMENT" app scans by CAR until the order is completed, as shown in the "RECEIVE PRODUCT" app?

Answer: There is no single CBC checkbox that makes RF behave like Receive Products “scan = plus 1”. The options are to use an RF step that supports GTIN based product identification and confirm quantities separately, or you continue using Receive Products for scan-increment receiving.

It is recommended to:

  • Use an RF step that supports “scan product”, not CHQTY. In EWM RF, you need a step where the RF screen has a Product/Barcode/GTIN input filed verification, then the system can determine the product based on GTIN. If your current flow is Receiving HU by Delivery à Select HU à CHQTY, you are already at a quantity correction screen and not a “scan product” screen.
  • Check whether your RF menu has a receiving step that supports product identification scanning. Ther RF framework supports barcode validation, but id depends on the RF transaction. See Radio Frequency Framework for more details.

If your RF content only offer CHQTY at that point, then the standard RF flow you are using does not support scanning a GTIN to increment quantity.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Error during Portugal Settlement Document Creation –...
entry.content:
I believe, the process remains same for On-Premise also

  • In On‑Premise, you configure number ranges and assign them to country PT (as you already did).
  • However, the validation code must still be maintained in the customizing for the series.
  • SAP delivers the framework, but the validation code itself comes only after registration with AT.
  • There is no “test bypass” once digital signature is active — the system checks for the validation code every time.

entry.title: SAC: Story fails to connect when opening from URL

A client can open and consume a story normally when he opens the story from the folder where it is located.

He tried, with equal results, these two URLs to the story:

- the one he copied from the browser when successfully opening the story from the folder (ending with [story UID]/?mode=view)

We need direct links to the story (used to open from Fiori).

Any idea what could be the problem?

Thanks for your inputs!

Stéphane

entry.title: SAP DM - Global variable memory consumption with different data type
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we want to use the global variable to hold data that is quite large in volume. but as per SAP documentation global variable can hold only 2 MB of data in it so we want to make sure that if we use global variable to hold data which data type we should use so it can hold more data. 

as per our understanding and checked : 

* String - it will take less memory and more data in terms of storing data. but data extraction logic will be complex.

* structure: it will hold less data and take more memory. but data extraction logic can be easy.

has anyone tried using structure array and structure Map in terms of how much data they can hold if we use global variable of these type.

 this is the sample payload we want to store in global variable:

{
"partSerialNumber": "1234",
"inventoryID": "ABC",
"batchNumber": "SDFS",
"status": "HOLD"
}

if anyone has worked with these type please help us to understand, quick response is appreciated.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Curious about SAP’s latest path to Concur request certification?
entry.content:
We are excited to share that SAP Learning has released a brand‑new Learning Journey Becoming a Certified Configuration Administrator in Concur Request Professional Edition  designed for everyone who works with in an administration or configuration role.

This offering provides a complete, role‑based path to help you:

  • Understand the foundational features of Concur Request
  • Create, manage, and approve request workflows
  • Apply travel segments and expected expenses within the system
  • Build and optimize approval workflows
  • Use the configuration and administration tools necessary to effectively manage Concur Request environments 

This journey aligns perfectly with administrators seeking to deepen their expertise and build the skills needed for certification.

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • Concur Request Administrators
  • Configuration Specialists
  • Implementation or Support Consultants
  • Anyone aiming to become certified on Concur Request Professional Edition

If you have prior experience using Concur Request as a business user, this journey will help you take the next step toward expert-level configuration and administration.

entry.title: Join SAP at the Future of Utilities Energy Transition Summit in Amsterdam
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that we will be joining the Future of Utilities: Energy Transition Summit 2026 as a Platinum Sponsor. The event takes place March 18–19, 2026 at the SugarFactory in Amsterdam, bringing together Europe’s most influential leaders shaping the future of energy.

This summit has a great reputation for real conversations, not just presentations. It’s a chance to learn from each other, exchange ideas, and meet people facing the same challenges and opportunities across Europe’s evolving energy landscape.

SAP on Stage: Opening the Conversation on Resilience

We’re also proud that Kim Maren Ekrutt will take part in the Leaders Forum during the opening session:
“Towards a Robust and Resilient Energy Ecosystem – Mapping the Blueprint”

🗓March 18, 2026
⏰10:15 CET

This discussion kicks off the summit and dives into important topics such as resilience, grid modernization, and how all of us—from utilities to regulators to technology providers—can work together to build a stronger and more flexible energy system

Come Meet Us at the SAP Booth

Throughout the event, our SAP Utilities experts will be at the booth and around the venue. If you’re attending, we’d really love to connect, whether you’re:

  • Modernizing your grid
  • Integrating more renewables
  • Exploring flexibility solutions
  • Rethinking customer engagement
  • Dealing with regulatory or market change
  • Or simply curious what others in the industry are doing

If you’re curious about how AI is reshaping the utilities landscape, we’d love to show you what’s possible with SAP Business AI.
We’re always up for a conversation—whether it’s about new use cases, lessons learned, ongoing projects, or ideas you’re exploring but haven’t kicked off yet. And if you’re looking for peers tackling similar issues, we’re happy to help make introductions.

 
On top of that, we’re happy to treat you to a freshly brewed coffee from our barista bar—perfect fuel for great conversations.

Let’s Shape What’s Next Together

The energy transition is a team effort, and this summit is one of those rare moments when so many people across the industry come together to compare notes, challenge ideas, and spark new thinking.

SAP is glad to be part of the conversation, and we’re genuinely looking forward to meeting familiar faces and new ones alike.

If you want to take a look at the full agenda or learn more about the event, you can find all the details here:
futureofutilities.com/events/energy-transition-summit

👉Learn more about the event:
futureofutilities.com/events/energy-transition-summit

 
Let’s connect over the app and meet in Amsterdam!

entry.title: Unsupported Data Type DECFLOAT16 in RAP Service Binding – SAP S/4HANA On-Premise
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently developing a RAP (RESTful Application Programming) report in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise to send data from SAP to a third-party system using CPI. In this report, I am performing calculations based on different functional scenarios.

In one scenario, I need to compare two custom fields (from custom tables). One field has the data type CHAR(16) and the other has the data type DF16_DEC.

When I try to compare them directly in Eclipse, I get a syntax error due to the data type mismatch. To resolve this, I converted both fields to DECFLOAT16 using a CASE statement. With this approach, the syntax error is resolved.

However, when I create a service binding on top of the service consumption model, I receive the following error:

Unsupported data type DECFLOAT16 for elements.

Could you please share your suggestions on how to handle this issue properly in RAP? Also, if you have any helpful blogs or references that address this scenario, I would greatly appreciate it.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP and BlackLine: Enhancing the Gold Standard Partnership with AI Capabilities
entry.content:
As the use of AI becomes common practice among finance and accounting teams, SAP and BlackLine are reviewing capabilities to reduce the time and effort our customers spend on financial closing and record-to-report (R2R) activities, all while upholding the highest standards of integrity and compliance.

Building on the gold standard our partnership has set for record-to-report (R2R), we are now exploring how  the capabilities of SAP Joule and BlackLine Verity AI can work together to enhance the gold standard for our joint customers.

While SAP has tens of thousands of partners, only a few dozen have reached the status of solution extension. At this partnership level, SAP and BlackLine product teams review select and relevant development plans and product roadmaps to ensure alignment and interoperability.  When companies choose SAP and BlackLine to support their mission critical financial close and record-to-report activities, they are choosing the gold standard.

This collaboration can unlock greater efficiency and productivity enabling customers to achieve more. By aligning technologies and expertise, both SAP and BlackLine are committed to delivering enhanced AI products that work better together to deliver value and help drive transformative outcomes for finance teams worldwide. We look forward to providing further updates as our joint work on AI progresses.

Learn more about SAP solutions for accounting automation here:

https://www.sap.com/products/financial-management/account-substantiation-automation-blackline.html

entry.title: Unable to Update Data in Custom External S/4HANA OData Service (SSAM2405)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are trying to integrate an additional external S/4HANA OData service (our own custom service) into the standard SAP Service and Asset Manager (SSAM) 2405 application.

Steps we followed:

  1. Created a destination in SAP Mobile Services for our external S/4HANA service.

     2. Added the service to the standard SSAM metadata by creating a new service in the Z component  

           

     3. After synchronization, the service metadata was generated successfully (XML created without errors)

     4. We are able to read data from the backend service successfully in the application

Issue:

When we try to update data from the Edit page, the Save action triggers the OData Update action, but the data is not updated. We are receiving an Internal Server Error.

In the backend traces, only GET requests are being triggered. There are no POST or PUT requests being sent during the update operation.

 

Question:

Is it supported to use a custom external S/4HANA OData service within the standard SSAM metadata?

If yes:

  • Are there any additional configuration steps required to update the data? Have we missed anything?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you in advance.

Best regards,
Kingston A.

entry.title: Emarsys contact deduplication
entry.content:
Hi, I have been following the webinar series from https://community.sap.com/t5/customer-experience-learning-group-blog-posts/live-session-get-certified-sap-certified-associate-implementation/ba-p/14313875 but I have some doubts regarding the session about "SAP Emarsys - From Data to Delivery: Mastering Contact Sync and Email Templates".

It is unclear to me what SAP Emarsys can do when it comes to contact deduplication.

In the webinar, it appeared as if only ONE single Unique ID could be chosen for SAP Emarsys. And you have to choose between either Email OR CustomerID. And the choice has a big impact on the features that you can use in Emarsys, i.e. choosing for email will not allow you to use Web Push. 

On the other hand, on https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EMARSYS/f8e2fafeea804018a954a8857d9dfff3/fdf598a674c11014adc1d1efe5e1d939.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST there is a section that indicates "When you use manual or automatic contact import in SAP Emarsys, you can select the database fields against which duplication checks are run. These are called Unique Identifiers and can be defined after you have selected the import file you want to upload. The system will treat contacts as duplicates if all the fields marked as unique identifiers match those of an existing contact."

So how does that deduplication handling then connect to the choice of the Unique ID of the Emarsys system?

And how is the deduplication handling working when contact data is integrated via API? Or via a standard connector package, like the "Account Engagement" connector for SAP Sales Cloud v1? (especially in SAP Sales Cloud it often happens that contacts have the same email address, but they have different business partner ids, so based on the business partner id they could be identified as unique contacts. But the Sales Cloud bupa id is not the only identfier that a contact can have, they can also have an identifier id from sap commerce, from sap erp, etc) .

 

Thanks if anyone can clear this up 🙂 I wanted to contact the trainer from the webinar Alexandre Robin but I can't seem to find him on Community to tag.

entry.title: Quick insight on SAP BTP Document Management service for managing and transformation of Documents
entry.content:
In this blog, I would like to share the functional aspects of SAP Document management service. The main focus is to understand deployment scenarios, capabilities comparison and key features.

  1. Introduction
  2. Key Features
  3. Service Options
  4. Capabilities comparison between three options
  5. Integration and Deployment options
  6. Monitoring and Troubleshooting 
  7. Future Roadmap
  8. Conclusion
  9. Additional Information

Introduction: 

In today's digital business environment, organization are generating and managing large volume of documents across different operations from engineering drawings, CAD files, PO (Purchase Orders), Regulatory documents, etc. Effective document management has become a critical success factor for enterprise seeking operational efficiency, regulatory compliance and audit purpose. For e.g. Pharma. A&D industry have to maintain these documents for multiple decades for regulatory purposes. 

Not only this, but business users also want search capabilities to search documents based on Meta Data as well as content within the document, easy access, authorization control and lift and shift of the repository independent of the application solution. 

SAP Document Management service emerges as a comprehensive solution designed to address these challenges, providing organizations with robust capabilities to store, organize and manage their business-critical documents within the SAP ecosystem. 

What is SAP Document Management Services?

SAP Document Management Service is a content management solution available on the Cloud Foundry environment of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It is based on the OASIS (Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards) industry standard CMIS (Content Management interoperability Services) and include features like versioning, Hierarchies, access control and document lifecycle. 

Key Features: 

1. Connect to your business storage:

Bring your choice of CMIS-compliant, on-premises or cloud repository to connect with SAP Document Management Service and leverage its capabilities.

2. Embed with your business applications: 

Leverage the APIs to build a customized document management layer on your on-premises or cloud applications. Embed an easy-to-use, UI5-based, reusable component into your application for document management scenarios.

3. Manage enterprise content:

Organize your enterprise content with the ready-to-use SAP Fiori web application and take advantage of document management capabilities like file and folder hierarchy, versioning, authority management, status management, etc.

Service Options: 

The SAP Document Management service offers the following options:

  • Document Management Service, Integration Option
  • Document Management Service, Application Option
  • Document Management Service, Repository Option

Capabilities comparison between three options:

 

Capabilities

Integration

Application

Repository

Service type

PaaS

SaaS

Optional service

Primary purpose

Integrate document management capabilities into business application

Provide a ready-to-use application for document management

Provide storage in the cloud for other options

Ideal for

Companies who want to add document management capabilities to business application via APIs

Companies who want Out-of-the-box document management application

Customer who needs storage for application or integration option.

APIs Provided

Yes

Not directly but can be used via integration option

Used only for storage, not standalone APIs

UI Availability

Reusable UI component

Comes with a Ready to use application

No UI

Storage Included

No

No

Yes

Used Standalone

Yes

 Yes

No

Relationship between Options

Included with Application option automatically

Must link to integration option to use APIs

Only can be used with integration or application

Cloud Cockpit Tiles

Visible as a Service

Visible as an Application Service

No tiles shown — entitlement must be manually added

Integration and Deployment options

Document management service, Integration Options: You can use Integration service for option below.

  1. Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  2. Integrating SAP S/4HANA with Google Workspace using SAP Document Management services
  3. Integration with Document Grounding service to allow it to gather information from Documents and make it available as an AI source.

Initial Setup for Integration option:

  1. Create a subaccount and space
  2. Create and update a service instance and service key
  3. Authorization

Initial Setup for application option:

  1. Creating a Subaccount
  2. Subscribing to Document Management Service, Application Option
  3. Configuring User Access
  4. Accessing the Application

Monitoring and Troubleshooting 

If you encounter any issue related to the SAP Document Management Service product, we recommend the following options to troubleshoot the issue.

1. Check Platform Status

Check the availability of the platform at SAP Trust Center Information published on SAP site.

2. Check Guided Answers

In the SAP Support Portal, check the Guided Answers Information published on SAP site section for SAP BTP.

3. Create SAP incident

You can report an incident or error using SAP component i.e. BC-CP-CF-SDM*. Under SDM you can select Integration, application or repository options.

Future Roadmap

The SAP Document Management Service continues to evolve with enhanced AI and machine learning capabilities for document classification and extraction using Document grounding services. 

Integration extension plans include enhanced connectivity with third-party systems and improved mobile capabilities. The roadmap also emphasizes advanced analytics and reporting features to provide insights into document usage and compliance metrics.

Conclusion

  • SAP Document Management Service represents a strategic investment in digital transformation, providing organizations with the tools needed to effectively manage their document lifecycle's while maintaining security, compliance, and operational efficiency.
  • SAP Document Management Service is a content management solution on the Cloud Foundry environment of SAP BTP.
  • You can consume the SAP Document Management Service in different ways:
    • SAP Document Management service, integration option lets you build document management capabilities for your business applications.
    • SAP Document Management service, application option is a stand-alone, ready-to-use web application that provides document management capabilities for your enterprise content.
    • SAP Document Management service, repository option lets you securely store and manage your documents.
    • SAP Document Management Service desktop client is a single app and lets you keep your files in sync with your device files.

Additional Resources

 SAP Help Portal - Document Management Service

What's New for Document Management Service

Feel free to post your comments and I encourage you to post questions about the topic in our SAP Community using this link.

Stay Tuned!

Thanks
Amit Dhar, SAP PLM Solution Architect/Expert

entry.title: Reintroducing the SAP Cloud ALM Implementation Expert Portal
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ALM Implementation Expert Portal has been redesigned to make it easier for you to find the guidance you need to work effectively with SAP Cloud ALM. 
 
The page now has a clearer structure and a stronger focus on bringing Cloud ALM best practices front and center — so you can quickly understand the processes for using it and dive into the areas that matter most for your role.
 
What is it?

The SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation Expert Portal provides deep‑dive knowledge and guidance to help you start working in SAP Cloud ALM. The content is curated by the product team and complementary to the Help Portal. On the expert portal you can:

  • Explore content through best‑practice processes and project roles to understand the end‑to‑end implementation flow
  • Or browse by capability to access the latest updates, expert insights, and guidance

What's changed?

1. Best practice featured at the top

The expert portal now opens with the end-to-end Implementation best practice, the complete process showing how Cloud ALM is used across the full implementation. This is your starting point for understanding the overall flow before diving into individual areas of work. The best practice process can also serve as the foundation for identifying and adapting the process to your needs, which can be done directly in Cloud ALM in a fit-to-standard workshop. If you need access to powerpoint versions of the best practice description guides, you can request via the instruction on the expert portal.

2. One end-to-end best practice process, delivered as modular subprocess guides

The end‑to‑end best practice is made up of multiple subprocesses, and each subprocess now has its own focused best practice description guide. This new structure replaces the older single large deck and makes it easier to:

  • Pick up the subprocess you’re working on right now
  • Prepare for upcoming phases of your project
  • Share only the relevant content with your team
  • Navigate process guidance without scrolling through unrelated material

All solution‑specific or integration‑specific variations are flagged clearly within each subprocess guide, so differences are visible without maintaining separate decks.

 
This modular format also allows us to update individual subprocess guides more rapidly whenever guidance evolves rather than waiting to refresh an entire document.

3. Capability based Expert content for deeper exploration
Below the best practices, you'll find the Expert Content section organized by capability.  It's designed both to help you dive deeper into topics related to the best practice guides and to give experienced users a quick way to jump straight to a specific capability they use for their role.

To make changes easier to spot, we have indicators for content that is new or updated. For this first publication, the indicator highlights new content from the last six months. As we continue to maintain the content, the indicator will only appear for items that have been added or revised since the most recent validity review for that capability.

4. Guidance that complements the Help Portal
As part of the redesign, content that described product features and functions was removed from the Expert Portal. This information lives in the SAP Help Portal, where it remains complete, accurate, and up to date. The Expert Portal goes beyond what is documented in the Help Portal and focuses on implementation guidance, process context, role responsibilities and practical considerations for using Cloud ALM. When details about features or configuration are needed, the Expert Portal content links directly to the relevant Help Portal pages so you always have the right information at the right time.

How to use it

  1. Start with the end‑to‑end best practice to understand the full Cloud ALM implementation flow.
  2. Open the subprocess tab for the area you’re working on or preparing for to access the subprocess diagram and the corresponding best practice description guide. The tab also highlights which capabilities have related Expert Content available and shows where additional roles may be involved based on the activities described in the guide.
  3. Use the subprocess guide to review activities, decision points, and any flagged solution‑specific or integration‑specific considerations.
  4. Scroll to Expert Content for deeper insight into capabilities connected to the subprocess or your role.

Explore and provide feedback

The updated structure is designed to make it easier to navigate Cloud ALM implementation guidance and quickly find the information that matters most. It also provides a strong baseline for future improvements. If you notice anything missing or have suggestions, please share your feedback in the comments below.

Visit the updated SAP Cloud ALM Implementation Expert Portal

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

New POD 2.0 Framework

  1. The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
  2. Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
  3. Visual WYSIWYG Designer
  4. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 1
  5. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 2
  6. Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes

The next post on the series will be published as soon as this one reaches 20 Likes, so please leave a Like! 👍

🗓️ Timeline

POD 2.0 is currently in BETA with selected customers, General Availability (GA) is planned for 2605 release. BETA content is subject to change!

📢 Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes

In this blog, I will guide you in a very detailed step-by-step on how to use the brand-new POD 2.0 framework to create custom Dialog UIs that can collect worker inputs, call a Production Process and display the output.

This step-by-step example will be built on top the following use case: Consume unplanned non-BOM components in a ETO (Engineer-to-Order) manufacturing process.

Out-of-the-box, the 'Component Consumption' plugin, which by the way is now harmonized between Work Center, Operation and Order PODs, only support the consumption of planned components, components which are in the BOM.

However, in many cases, consuming unplanned non-BOM components is a very common, simple and valid requirement.

So, let´s see how to implement that using latest POD 2.0 Framework capabilities.

Prerequisites

The use case implementation below leverages a Production Processes described here: Assembly of Unplanned (non-BOM) Materials, so if you want to implement the same use case, check this blog post for more details.

Let´s Build

First, create a new or open an existing POD in Manage PODs 2.0 app. In 'Pages' tab on the left-hand side, click '+' and 'Add Dialog'.

Give it a name, and click 'OK'.

Adjust the Dialog look and feel on the right-hand side panel, like Title, Dimensions, etc.

Then, drag and drop following 'Elements' from the left-hand side panel o the 'Dialog': Two 'Input' elements and one 'Text':

For the 1st 'Input', adjust 'Description' to 'Material' and 'Value' to: {/nomBOMAssy/input/material}

For the 2nd 'Input', adjust 'Description' to 'Quantity' and 'Value' to: {/nomBOMAssy/input/qty}

Finally, for the 'Text', adjust 'Text' to: {/nomBOMAssyOutput/output}

Now, click on the existing 'Close' button on the bottom-right corner of the Dialog, adjust 'Text' to "Assemble" and click 'Edit Actions'.

Click '+' ('Add Action') and select 'Production Process' as 'Action', give it an 'Action ID' and click 'Add'.

Click on the newly added action on the left-hand side panel and use 'arrow up' to move it up, before the 'CloseDialog'.

On the right-hand side panel, browse and select your production process, and map the production process input and output parameters. In my case, I´m using the Production Process previously described here: Assembly of Unplanned (non-BOM) Materials

Note that many production process input parameters are mapped to POD Variables, like Operation Activity, Plant, Resource and SFC. Others, like inComponent and inQty are mapped to the 'Input' element values that we created and defined previously.

Finally, set 'Store Output as New Variable' as 'nomBOMAssyOutput', which is where the production process output will be stored so it can be displayed in the 'Text' element we created and defined previously.

In order to see the 'Text' element displaying the production process output value, click 'CloseDialog' and then the 'Trash' bin icon to delete 'CloseDialog' action, as we don´t want the Dialog to close automatically after the component is assembled, we want that the dialog remains open instead.

To address the manual dialog closure, add a 'Button' element to the dialog, besides the existing 'Assemble' button as follows:

Adjust 'Text' to 'Close' and click 'Edit Actions' and '+' to add a 'CloseDialog' action.

Now that the dialog is ready, we have to display it.

On 'SFCDetails' page, select 'Component Consumption' tab.

From 'Plugins' tab, drag and drop a 'Button' element, adjust 'Text' to 'Consume Unplanned Components', and click 'Edit Actions'.

Here, click '+' as before, and add a 'ShowDialog' action.

Then, map the dialog you just created.

WE ARE DONE!

Just hit 'Save' and 'Run' ('Play' button) in the top-right corner, so you can give it a try.

Once the POD loads, use Work Center and Resource filters to find and open any SFC.

Then, move to 'Component Consumption' tab to see the newly created 'Consume Unplanned Components' custom button.

Once you click 'Consume Unplanned Components' button, the custom dialog will show, where you just need to enter the required data and click 'Assemble' to call the production process passing all the mapped input values.

The production process output is shown in the 'Text' element added below the 'Input' fields, and you can click 'Close' to close the dialog.

*** A 'Refresh Component List' is not yet available in BETA, but once available in the future, it could be also triggered when pressing the 'Close' dialog button.

As an automatic refresh of the 'Component Consumption' is not yet possible, you must manually uncheck and check again the operation from the Operation Activity List or manually refresh the POD, to force a refresh of the 'Component List', so you can see the newly assembled unplanned component showing up in the bottom of the 'Component List'.

The next post on the series will be published as soon as this one reaches 20 Likes, so please leave a Like! 👍

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: How to Report and Steer Sustainability with SAP Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
Why SAP Sustainability Control Tower Matters

SAP Sustainability Control Tower allows companies to:

  • Track sustainability performance across all business areas in real time
  • Align ambitions with measurable targets and KPIs
  • Integrate data from multiple SAP and external systems for a complete view
  • Ensure transparency and compliance with sustainability reporting standards
  • Turn insights into actionable initiatives that reduce environmental impact and optimize operations

By providing a centralized, structured platform for sustainability data, SAP Sustainability Control Tower helps organizations move from spreadsheets and siloed reporting to strategic, data-driven sustainability management.

Key Features of SAP Sustainability Control Tower

  • Centralized Data Management: Collect, harmonize, and manage sustainability data from multiple SAP and non-SAP systems.
  • Performance Tracking: Define measures, dimensions, and metrics to track environmental and operational performance in real time.
  • Target Setting and Monitoring: Set ambitions and KPIs and monitor progress toward organizational sustainability goals.
  • Integration with SAP Solutions: Seamlessly connect with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, SAP EHS Management, and SAP Datasphere.
  • Reporting and Transparency: Generate actionable insights and support reporting for internal stakeholders and regulatory compliance.
  • APIs for Data Exchange: Use inbound and outbound APIs for smooth integration with other systems and automated data flows..

Who Should Take the Course?

  • Business Users and Administrators: Manage sustainability data and track performance across your organization.
  • Sustainability Managers: Monitor environmental and operational metrics, set targets, and drive measurable progress.
  • SAP Consultants: Support implementation and integration of sustainability solutions for clients.
  • Developers: Work with APIs and system integrations to ensure accurate and seamless sustainability data flows.

No prior experience is required, making it accessible for beginners while still providing value for those with some SAP knowledge. By completing this course, professionals gain the skills to turn sustainability ambitions into actionable results.

Start your journey toward data-driven sustainability management today and explore the Learning Journey here.

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for Service PO Pricing Matrix (Thicknes...
entry.content:
Best Practice considerations in S/4HANA

Standard practice: For subcontracting/external processing, SAP typically recommends using a service material (DIEN) or a subcontracting material with conditions.

Multiple address/service materials are not mandatory, but they improve automation and reporting.

Contracts with multiple items are valid, but manual selection is not ideal for operational efficiency.

Options for Automating Price Determination

1) Condition Technique (Purchasing Info Records / Contracts)
You can use condition tables and access sequences to drive price determination based on material characteristics. However, thickness/width are not standard fields in purchasing conditions — you’d need custom fields or classification.

2) Classification + Variant Conditions
Create characteristics for thickness and width. Use variant conditions in pricing to determine the correct price automatically. This requires some customizing but avoids creating 80 materials.

3) Custom Development (BAdI/Enhancement)
If standard condition technique cannot handle thickness/width, a BAdI can be used to derive the correct price line based on entered values. This is common in industries with matrix pricing.

Practical Recommendation

  • If reporting and automation are important → create separate service materials per combination.
  • If simplicity of master data is more important → use one DIEN material with contract lines, but accept manual selection.
  • For true automation without 80 materials → consider classification + variant conditions or a custom BAdI to map thickness/width to price.

entry.title: Re: Maintaining of Usage attribute
entry.content: Even if the producer sells packaging to another company, if that packaging is ultimately used for consumer goods, the Usage Attribute should be “Household.” Only packaging that never reaches the consumer market (purely industrial/commercial use) should be marked “Commercial.”

entry.title: Re: PR/PO Flexible Workflow gets stuck (SAP_WFRT)
entry.content:
Good day @env9710A 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your log and SAP_WFRT as processor, this looks like a workflow runtime callback failure which is often triggered by concurrent processing/locking while the background job is being executed. SAP_WFRT is the technical workflow runtime user, not the root cause in this case. KBA 3556752 describes this pattern. You should expand your callback error, and you will typically see the real reason. Also, it is expected that the PO can remain in Approval while the workflow status is Error.

The supported standard for recovery is via Flexible Workflow Administration. Where you can resume one or several instances.

The recommended approach for high volume is to prevent the lock situation by making sure job execution is sequenced, so follow up jobs do not update document while workflow callbacks are being finalised. You can also monitor by using the workflow troubleshooting apps.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4de200b26d5d42bbacae69c6bf6b9923/297b2b2d40ed42d5b674d8b04ff17021.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/10e7e36908184328aab7d5007d9ecb13.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/2966c6fc0b2c4667bd1511f974f187fe.html?locale=en-US

Below are answers to your questions:

Question: Is there a known root cause / fix for PR/PO workflows getting stuck with SAP_WFRT in Public Cloud?

Answer: As mentioned above SAP_WFRT itself is not the issue. Problem is the workflow runtime user. The issues lie with the callback failing. You can fix it by

  1. Identifying the real error by expanding your workflow log. In your case it was because “Sales Document 368” was already being processed
  2. Reducing concurrency in your integration chain

Question: Is there any supported way to automate recovery, e.g., a scheduled job/template/API to detect + resume stuck workflow instances?

Answer: In Public Cloud, the Flexible Workflow Administration is the supported way to handle it. You can resume one or several workflow instances via the app.

Question: If auto-resume isn’t supported, what’s SAP’s recommended approach for high-volume integration to avoid integration stoppage (monitoring/alerts/config patterns)?

Answer: recommendation is for you to use the delivered troubleshooting apps and design the integration to avoid lock collisions. Preventively you can:

  1. Serialise critical steps and ensure you do not run PR àPO conversion and follow up custom jobs in overlapping windows for the same document set.
  2. If the standard jobs create multiple POs per item, reduce parallel updates downstream until workflow is completed.
  3. Only trigger follow up jobs once PO approval status is final and workflow is not in error.
  4. Consider introducing alerts when your workflows enter Error, so your support acts before the process chain is blocked.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP PAPM - Disable "Erase data" for End users
entry.content:
Hi @DSA25 

Question from me would be, why do we want to restrict/disable "Erase data" feature when you said end users are adjusting some data? Do you mean adjusting does not include deleting the data? What if data ends up to be invalid, then adjustments like deleting this data will not be possible 😉

Although, if you are referring to restricting the deletion on a specific line of data, then maybe you can consider 'Data Privilege'?

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-profitability-and-performance-management-cloud-analytic-privileges/ba-p/13535478 

Also we have what we call Data Slice Locks https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-sap/data-management-screen-managing-analytic-privileges-fix-selections-amp-data/ba-p/14177609 

You may explore these blogpost to achieve this 😉

entry.title: Re: Integrating low code solutions with Microsoft using SAP Integration Suite has never been easier!
entry.content:
Hi @Martin-Pankraz 

Thanks again for your prompt response. The thing in our case it seems is that EntraId is generating a SAML Assertion instead a JWT token for the Caller app ( I don´t really know if it is possible because I don´t have enough EntraId knowlodge) . Because of this I asked you if this flow could be valid without a JWT and using as inbound in SAP APIM a SAML assertion.

It would be like starting at this point?

3. Token exchange of SAML2 assertion issued by Entra ID to SAP access token issued by SAP OAuth2 server. The request is invoked by API Management. The result is a token carrying the authorizations set on the SAP backend (PFCG transaction) for that end user.

Thanks in advance,

Regards

Jorge

entry.title: Re: In Datasphere Analytic Model, can I have two structur...
entry.content: In SAP Datasphere, you cannot directly create two separate structures like BW’s “rows vs columns” approach. You can achieve similar results by using a combination of restricted key figures, calculated key figures, and multiple calculated measures within the same analytic model. Apply filters for row-related dimensions in one set of measures and date-related dimensions for columns. Layouts can then display these measures in a pivot-like visualization, giving a “rows vs columns” effect.

entry.title: I finally found a use for AI in Datasphere
entry.content:
Introduction

Welcome to yet another blog post where I try to close the gab between Datasphere and BW. 

In this blog, however, my main goal is to shine a spotlight on a very small, but important part of the Datasphere solution, that could be infinitely improved, if someone has 5 minutes to spare at SAP, when they're done trying to introduce AI generated semantics, or some other buzzword soup.

Today, ladies and gentlemen, we're talking about calendar translation tables - so buckle up! 
You're in for a ride.

Background

So, until recently, you might not ever have heard of Denmark. We're a small kingdom in Scandinavia, and we have our own language, but since we're home to around 6 mio. people, we grew up with subtitles in all movies and TV, and if you wanted to read the latest books, you had to order an English version, rather than wait 6 months for the translation.

So, why do I bring that up? 
Because most software - especially for public sector clients -  in Denmark has to be available in Danish as well. 

Which brings us to SAP and Datasphere.

Like most of their solutions, Datasphere is available in up to 40 languages, and even allows users to choose between dialects for large languages like Portuguese, French, Chinese  and English.

Hell, you can have the UI in one language, and the data in another - how cool is that!? 

The problem

Okay, so let's get into the issue here. One issue is the translation services, which is going to be another blog post entirely, I promise.
But for the actual data, the dimension-to-text-entity paradigme works fine - until you're missing a text in the selected data language, in which case it'll just display the key, which is also fine. In most cases you can go back to whatever source you have and create the missing texts, and ✨ tadaaaaa ✨ now the texts are there. 

Except for one case . The time dimensions. 

Datasphere allows you to, on a given space, generate time dimension data, which you can then use to model your time dimensions. 
When you do that, you also get three translation tables, Day, Month, and Quarter, that provides the texts for all the supported data languages- right?

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Nope. 

Well, obviously we can just open the tables and add new records? 

Nope. 

I managed to find an SAP Note, which - I have to say - is pure comedy gold. 
3288674 - Translation Table Missing Languages - SAP for Me

"The translation tables are automatically created by SAP Datapshere for the EN, ES, FR and DE languages and it is not able to edit or add more to it."

Yep, you read that right. If your user has their data access language set to anything except English, French, Spanish, or German - you just get the keys. 

Okay, what the hell. Guess I'll just copy the tables, add my own values and union them in a view - bit of a pain in the backside, but I can do that at least, right?

You guessed it - nope. You can't copy it. 

So, if you are from a small kingdom in Scandinavia, working for a local public sector client, you have to create it all on your own. 

So why only those four languages?

And okay, I admit, that's not too much work - will take you half an hour at most to get everything sorted out. 
Why, oh why Christian are you so upset?

Surely SAP has very good, logical, technically complex reason why they only support those four languages, right? 

See, that's what I thought as well. I had several theories, including the locales installed on the OS the server was running on.

But no. No no no. I'll show you. And I'll show you how to check that I'm not making it up. Because it sounds made up. 

So, first of all to replicate the solution, you have to create a DB-user on your Datasphere tenant, like described in this blog post, and then open the Database Explorer:

From there, open the Catalog, selection functions, limit the results to the "DWC_GLOBAL" schema and search for "GENERATE". 

Open up any of the _LOOKUP functions, and select the "CREATE" statement tab. 

And now you see. 

 

It's just a hardcoded list. 
The reason you have to spend your valuable time on this earth create copies of tables and uploading your values, is because someone only bothered to hardcode four languages. 

Does that not raise your pulse a bit? 

Just wait until I tell you that this problem was already solved.
In all SAP system, you can find some standard tables. For months, open any SAP system and access table T247. It's all in there. 
It has been a solved problem for decades. 

Solution - Let's nuke the fridge with some AI, and some sarcasm.

Okay, so maybe the SAP developer didn't have access to an SAP system so check those tables. 
Or maybe it was just too much work to format the values into SQL. 
There could be many reasons. None of them are good. 

But okay, let's do all the cool kids, and not spend ½ an hour to format the SQL query, let's waste some water with AI.

So I basically just copy-pasted the SQL function, told it to add all the languages in the documentation, and ignore the ones in there. 
Oh, and I just used the Co-pilot embedded in my MSTeams client.

So, here you go SAP, have it all! 

-- FILEOWNER: [Provisioning, Spaces]
CREATE FUNCTION "DWC_GLOBAL"."_DIM_GENERATE_MONTH_LOOKUP"
(
  IN IV_LANGUAGE NVARCHAR(10)   -- <== enlarged from 5
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  "ID" NVARCHAR(2),
  "_DESCRIPTION" NVARCHAR(50),
  "_LANGU" NVARCHAR(10)         -- <== enlarged from 5
)
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
SQL SECURITY INVOKER AS
BEGIN
  DECLARE months TABLE("ID" NVARCHAR(2), "_DESCRIPTION" NVARCHAR(50), "_LANGU" NVARCHAR(10));

  -- EXISTING DATA (en, de, fr, es) ... keep your current inserts here
  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------
  -- [Your original EN/DE/FR/ES inserts not repeated for brevity]

  -------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- NEW LOCALES BELOW (CLDR "abbreviated" month names unless CLDR uses numeric+unit)
  -------------------------------------------------------------------

  -- bg-BG (Bulgarian)
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','ян.','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','февр.','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('03','март','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('04','апр.','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('05','май','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('06','юни','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('07','юли','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('08','авг.','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('09','септ.','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('10','окт.','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('11','ноем.','bg-BG'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('12','дек.','bg-BG'));

  -- ca-ES (Català)
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','gen.','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','febr.','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('03','març','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('04','abr.','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('05','maig','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('06','juny','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('07','jul.','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('08','ag.','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('09','set.','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('10','oct.','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('11','nov.','ca-ES'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('12','des.','ca-ES'));

  -- zh-CN (Chinese, Simplified) – CLDR uses numeric + 月
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','1月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','2月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('03','3月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('04','4月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('05','5月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('06','6月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('07','7月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('08','8月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('09','9月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('10','10月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('11','11月','zh-CN'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('12','12月','zh-CN'));

  -- zh-TW (Chinese, Traditional) – same pattern
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','1月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','2月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('03','3月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('04','4月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('05','5月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('06','6月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('07','7月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('08','8月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('09','9月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('10','10月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('11','11月','zh-TW'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('12','12月','zh-TW'));

  -- hr-HR (Croatian)
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','sij','hr-HR'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','velj','hr-HR'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('03','ožu','hr-HR'));

  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('10','lis','hr-HR'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('11','stu','hr-HR'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('12','pro','hr-HR'));

  -- cs-CZ (Czech)
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','led','cs-CZ'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','úno','cs-CZ'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('03','bře','cs-CZ'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('04','dub','cs-CZ'));

  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('12','pro','cs-CZ'));

  -- da-DK (Danish)
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','jan.','da-DK'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','feb.','da-DK'));

  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('12','dec.','da-DK'));

  -- nl-NL (Dutch - Netherlands)
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','jan.','nl-NL'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','feb.','nl-NL'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('03','mrt.','nl-NL'));

  -- et-EE (Estonian)

  -- vi-VN (Vietnamese) – CLDR short “thg N”

  -- cy-GB (Welsh) – CLDR-derived abbreviations
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('01','Ion','cy-GB'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('02','Chwef','cy-GB'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('03','Maw','cy-GB'));

  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('09','Medi','cy-GB'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('10','Hyd','cy-GB'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('11','Tach','cy-GB'));
  :months.("ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU").insert(('12','Rhag','cy-GB'));

  -- Return one language or all
  IF :IV_LANGUAGE IS NOT NULL THEN
    -- If provided language not available, fall back to EN
    SELECT DISTINCT "_LANGU" INTO IV_LANGUAGE DEFAULT 'en'
    FROM :months
    WHERE "_LANGU" = :IV_LANGUAGE;

    RETURN
      SELECT "ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU"
      FROM :months
      WHERE "_LANGU" = :IV_LANGUAGE;
  ELSE
    RETURN SELECT "ID","_DESCRIPTION","_LANGU" FROM :months;
  END IF;
END;

A cutting-edge AI code example, ready for your Datasphere solution! 
Please, can I have my allstar badge now? Can I open a pull request somewhere? 

Or maybe, maybe, we could not hardcode the bloody thing?

entry.title: Route Map – No possibility to select “specific user” in Collaboration Step
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In our Compensation process, we are using a route map that includes a Collaboration Step.
Within this step, we currently assign the HR Rep. role as well as a Custom Role.

During the ongoing cycle, we realized that for one particular worksheet we need to change the Custom Role assignment to a specific user.

However, we discovered that the Collaboration Step offers no option to select a “specific user”, neither for standard roles nor for custom roles.

 

My questions would be:
- Has anyone encountered this issue before?
- Is there a workaround or known solution that would allow assigning a specific user—ideally on a per‑worksheet basis—within a Collaboration Step?

Any experiences, tips, or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance,
Johannes

entry.title: Re: Variable Formula Exit G_SET_FORMULA_EXIT
entry.content: In SAP Report Painter, the error “Variable name '#U003' not possible” occurs because the new formula exits (U001–U003) are not registered in the table SETEXITST. To fix it, create entries for each exit in SETEXITST with the appropriate language (EN/DE). Make sure the exit class implements G_SET_FORMULA_EXIT correctly, then assign the variable in the report. This registers the exit and resolves the syntax error.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1
entry.content:
Preliminary version of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1.

You can find this preview on the current SAP Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Re: Close customer project from "Contract preparation" stage
entry.content:
Good day @Meenu_Wavie 

Thank you for your question.

The reason you cannot “close directly” from Contract Preparation is, in Public Cloud, the stage is not just a label. It drives the projects lifecycle control and the billing integration logic.

  • Moving a project to “In Execution” is the point where the project is expected to be billable. That is why the system pushes you to assign a billing element when you try to move forward.
  • The standard “closure” process is also stage driven. It is expected that a project is Completed first, then it can be Closed via the closing process. See KBA 3672731 for more details.

So, the requirement to assign billing elements is not because SAP wants you to bill, but because the execution and closing logic is built around the billing-based project lifecycle.

Also, billing related apps only fully work once the project is in execution. See Billing for Projects: FAQ for insights.

Depending on what your goal is, there are two options you may consider:

  • If your goal is to delete (option A) the project, then you will have to set it to “In Planning” status. KBA 2857228 highlights this.

If the system does not allow you to move it back to planning, it usually means there is already follow on data that prevents rollback. If the system does not allow you do that then,

  • Option B is to move the project to In Execution (requires billing setup, hence billing element), then move the project to completed an run the job template for Close Projects to enable you set it to Closed.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to deactivate EBRR for EPPM
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EPPM, you cannot directly delete a company code linked to an existing Revenue Recognition (EBRR) key. To deactivate EBRR, you need to first ensure no active postings exist for that key, then adjust the configuration in Maintenance of Revenue Recognition to remove or block its usage for new transactions. Direct deletion isn’t allowed once the key is used.

entry.title: Re: Request Support: Incoterms Field Mandatory in PO
entry.content:
Dear @VA19,

For Public Cloud the recommended approach is not classic configuration like Screen layout at document level. Instead, SAP provides a released field-control Badi for PO header or item fields in Public Edition, for example

  • MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE_H = Change of Field Control for Purchase Order Header
  • SAP states this Badi can change field control in PO apps and that the affected header fields can be set to hidden, read-only, mandatory or optional

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/4a3cd87985414d2c97ffa6b2ff4f0ab9.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/259a396e6bdb4d08b130049880a3920f.html

So:

1. Open Custom Logic

2. Implement Badi MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE_H or MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE

3. Set the Incoterms header field to mandatory in the relevant PO app scenario

There is also a second released Badi:

  • BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO = Check of Purchase Order Before Saving
  • SAP describes it as the Badi to implement your own checks before a purchase order is saved

Because of that the best clean-core design is usually:

  • Primary to use MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE_H to make the field mandatory on screen
  • Safety net to use BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO to raise an error on save if Incoterms is still blank for any edge case/API/background scenario

Recommended solution:

  • Use Badi MM_PUR_S4_PO_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE_H to make Incoterms mandatory at PO header level in the UI
  • Optionally add Badi BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO as a hard validation before save

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Maintaining of Usage attribute
entry.content: That's correct. I would add that in many cases the packaging producer is not obligated for EPR reporting, so the usage does not matter. But in the cases where the producer is obligated (such as CONAI or UK EPR, when the packaging is sold to small producer), the packaging producer should specify the usage attribute, based on the design of the packaging. If it is designed for household products, then it would be household usage. In some cases RDP uses the combination of "usage" (what was the packaging designed for) and "customer role" (who the packaging was sold to) to determine the waste stream in the report.
